<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Koyasu-jinja (Ise)]][[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Koyasu-jinja (Ise)]][[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Koyasu-jinja (Ise)]][[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]]
{{Infobox religious building | name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} | image = File:Koyasu Shrine, Ise.jpg | location = {{ill|Ujikan-chō, Ise|de|Ujikan-chō, Ise|ja|宇治館町|zh|宇治馆町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikan-chō, Ise|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.458472|N|136.724944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map = Japan Mie | map_label_position = bottom | deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} | status = Shrine under jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|de|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|zh|皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | founded = Unknown | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} (October 22) | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} }}
'''Koyasu Shrine''' (Koyasu-jinja, 子安神社) is a shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|de|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|zh|皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Jingu). It stands within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikan-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture. East of the Uji Bridge, near the guard post of the shrine’s officers, it enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|de|Ōyamazumi-Schrein (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|zh|大山祇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} alongside Ōyamazumi Shrine <ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎（2008）：115ページ</ref>.
As its name suggests, many visitors come here to pray for safe childbirth<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">学研パブリッシング（2013）：55ページ</ref>.
==Overview== Koyasu Shrine is located at the foot of Mount Kamiji (神路山) within the Inner Shrine precincts<ref name="uy">宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：16ページ</ref>. It ranks 30th among the thirty subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：15–16ページ</ref>. Small votive torii gates dedicated to prayers for safe childbirth are offered at the shrine front<ref name="ic2">伊勢文化舎（2008）：116ページ</ref><ref name="gk"/>. These torii are sold by Tawaradaya shop in Ujiurata 1-chōme<ref name="ic2-119">伊勢文化舎（2008）：119ページ</ref>.
The honden (main hall) is enclosed by a tamagaki fence. Unlike most sessha, massha, and subordinate shrines of Ise Jingu, which typically do not have offertory boxes<ref>伊勢文化舎（2008）：26ページ</ref>, Koyasu Shrine provides a saisen-bako at its front. A flower-shaped chozubachi (water basin) stands before the shrine building<ref name="ic"/>. Directly in front is Koyasu Shrine; behind it is Ōyamazumi Shrine<ref name="ic"/>.
The enshrined deity is Konohanasakuya-hime no Kami (木花開耶姫神)<ref name="ic2"/>. Originally believed to be the ujigami (tutelary deity) of Ujikan-chō<ref name="ic2"/>, she is the daughter of Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神) enshrined at Ōyamazumi Shrine<ref name="ic"/>. She is also the wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto (瓊瓊杵尊), said to have given birth to three children in the midst of a blazing fire<ref name="ic2"/><ref name="gk"/>. From this myth, people pray here for safe childbirth, child conception, match-making, and warding off misfortune<ref name="ic2"/><ref name="gk"/>. Other Ise Jingu shrines known for such worldly faith include {{ill|Yuta Shrine|de|Yuta-Schrein|ja|湯田神社|zh|湯田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|de|Sonai-Schrein|ja|園相神社|zh|园相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|de|Katada-Schrein|ja|堅田神社|zh|堅田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|de|Akasaki-Schrein|ja|赤崎神社|zh|赤崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="st">櫻井（1991）：294ページ</ref>.
==History== The date of foundation is unknown. It is not listed in the Engishiki nor the Enryaku gishikichō, suggesting it did not exist in ancient times<ref name="st-226-272">櫻井（1991）：266, 272ページ</ref>.
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Divinities removed from Ise Jingu’s jurisdiction all shrines not recorded in the two ancient texts, causing Ōyamazumi Shrine—sharing grounds with Koyasu Shrine—to lose its status<ref name="st"/>. The shrine administration of Ise Jingu later negotiated with the Shrine Bureau, and in 1900 (Meiji 33) the parishioners of Ōyamazumi Shrine petitioned for reinstatement. Their request was accepted, and Ōyamazumi Shrine along with its subordinate Koyasu Shrine and the combined grounds of approximately 174 tsubo (≈575 m²) were reabsorbed into the Inner Shrine in 1900<ref>櫻井（1991）：274ページ</ref>.
In March 1924 (Taishō 11), the shrine was rebuilt<ref name="uy"/>.
==Access== * By bus: From the south exit of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}} (JR Sangu Line) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|en|Ujiyamada Station|WD=Q7878210|lt=Ujiyamada Station|1=Ujiyamada Station}} (Kintetsu Yamada/Toba Line), take the Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae (Inner Shrine entrance), approx. 15 minutes<ref name="mkr">三重県観光連盟 “[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html 伊勢神宮（内宮）の観光施設・周辺情報 - 観光三重]” (accessed September 12 2013)</ref>. Buses also run from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|WD=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Toba Line. * By car: From {{ill|Ise-Nishi IC|de|Ise-West Autobahnanschlussstelle|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|zh|伊势西交流道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the Ise Expressway, take Route 32 south about 5 minutes, or from {{ill|Ise IC|de|Ise-Autobahnanschlussstelle|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|zh|伊势交流道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}} via National Route 23 south for about 5 minutes. Parking is available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Koyasu-jinja (Ise)]][[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Koyasu-jinja (Ise)]][[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Koyasu-jinja (Ise)]][[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]]
{{Infobox religious building | name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} | image = File:Koyasu Shrine, Ise.jpg | location = {{ill|Ujikan-chō, Ise|de|Ujikan-chō, Ise|ja|宇治館町|zh|宇治馆町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikan-chō, Ise|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11449448}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.458472|N|136.724944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map = Japan Mie | map_label_position = bottom | deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|qid=Q1781862|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} | status = Shrine under jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|de|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|zh|皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | founded = Unknown | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|qid=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} (October 22) | location = {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} }}
'''Koyasu Shrine''' (Koyasu-jinja, 子安神社) is a shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|de|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|zh|皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Jingu). It stands within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikan-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture. East of the Uji Bridge, near the guard post of the shrine’s officers, it enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|de|Ōyamazumi-Schrein (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|zh|大山祇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} alongside Ōyamazumi Shrine <ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎（2008）：115ページ</ref>.
As its name suggests, many visitors come here to pray for safe childbirth<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">学研パブリッシング（2013）：55ページ</ref>.
==Overview== Koyasu Shrine is located at the foot of Mount Kamiji (神路山) within the Inner Shrine precincts<ref name="uy">宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：16ページ</ref>. It ranks 30th among the thirty subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：15–16ページ</ref>. Small votive torii gates dedicated to prayers for safe childbirth are offered at the shrine front<ref name="ic2">伊勢文化舎（2008）：116ページ</ref><ref name="gk"/>. These torii are sold by Tawaradaya shop in Ujiurata 1-chōme<ref name="ic2-119">伊勢文化舎（2008）：119ページ</ref>.
The honden (main hall) is enclosed by a tamagaki fence. Unlike most sessha, massha, and subordinate shrines of Ise Jingu, which typically do not have offertory boxes<ref>伊勢文化舎（2008）：26ページ</ref>, Koyasu Shrine provides a saisen-bako at its front. A flower-shaped chozubachi (water basin) stands before the shrine building<ref name="ic"/>. Directly in front is Koyasu Shrine; behind it is Ōyamazumi Shrine<ref name="ic"/>.
The enshrined deity is Konohanasakuya-hime no Kami (木花開耶姫神)<ref name="ic2"/>. Originally believed to be the ujigami (tutelary deity) of Ujikan-chō<ref name="ic2"/>, she is the daughter of Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神) enshrined at Ōyamazumi Shrine<ref name="ic"/>. She is also the wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto (瓊瓊杵尊), said to have given birth to three children in the midst of a blazing fire<ref name="ic2"/><ref name="gk"/>. From this myth, people pray here for safe childbirth, child conception, match-making, and warding off misfortune<ref name="ic2"/><ref name="gk"/>. Other Ise Jingu shrines known for such worldly faith include {{ill|Yuta Shrine|de|Yuta-Schrein|ja|湯田神社|zh|湯田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|de|Sonai-Schrein|ja|園相神社|zh|园相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|de|Katada-Schrein|ja|堅田神社|zh|堅田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|de|Akasaki-Schrein|ja|赤崎神社|zh|赤崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="st">櫻井（1991）：294ページ</ref>.
==History== The date of foundation is unknown. It is not listed in the Engishiki nor the Enryaku gishikichō, suggesting it did not exist in ancient times<ref name="st-226-272">櫻井（1991）：266, 272ページ</ref>.
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Divinities removed from Ise Jingu’s jurisdiction all shrines not recorded in the two ancient texts, causing Ōyamazumi Shrine—sharing grounds with Koyasu Shrine—to lose its status<ref name="st"/>. The shrine administration of Ise Jingu later negotiated with the Shrine Bureau, and in 1900 (Meiji 33) the parishioners of Ōyamazumi Shrine petitioned for reinstatement. Their request was accepted, and Ōyamazumi Shrine along with its subordinate Koyasu Shrine and the combined grounds of approximately 174 tsubo (≈575 m²) were reabsorbed into the Inner Shrine in 1900<ref>櫻井（1991）：274ページ</ref>.
In March 1924 (Taishō 11), the shrine was rebuilt<ref name="uy"/>.
==Access== * By bus: From the south exit of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|qid=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}} (JR Sangu Line) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|en|Ujiyamada Station|qid=Q7878210|lt=Ujiyamada Station|1=Ujiyamada Station}} (Kintetsu Yamada/Toba Line), take the Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae (Inner Shrine entrance), approx. 15 minutes<ref name="mkr">三重県観光連盟 “[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html 伊勢神宮（内宮）の観光施設・周辺情報 - 観光三重]” (accessed September 12 2013)</ref>. Buses also run from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|qid=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Toba Line. * By car: From {{ill|Ise-Nishi IC|de|Ise-West Autobahnanschlussstelle|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|zh|伊势西交流道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10884865}} on the Ise Expressway, take Route 32 south about 5 minutes, or from {{ill|Ise IC|de|Ise-Autobahnanschlussstelle|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|zh|伊势交流道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10884857}} via National Route 23 south for about 5 minutes. Parking is available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
==References== {{Reflist}}
==Bibliography== * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929年1月20日、862 p. * 学研パブリッシング『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、薗田稔監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82 p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』国書刊行会、1991年11月30日、318 p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008年12月23日、151 p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (Koyasu-jinja, 子安神社) is a shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|de|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|zh|皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine of Ise Jingu). It stands within the precincts of the Inner Shrine in Ujikan-chō, Ise, Mie Prefecture. East of the Uji Bridge, near the guard post of the shrine’s officers, it enshrines {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|de|Ōyamazumi-Schrein (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|zh|大山祇神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} alongside Ōyamazumi Shrine <ref name="ic">伊勢文化舎（2008）：115ページ</ref>.
As its name suggests, many visitors come here to pray for safe childbirth<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">学研パブリッシング（2013）：55ページ</ref>.
==Overview== Koyasu Shrine is located at the foot of Mount Kamiji (神路山) within the Inner Shrine precincts<ref name="uy">宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：16ページ</ref>. It ranks 30th among the thirty subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine<ref>宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：15–16ページ</ref>. Small votive torii gates dedicated to prayers for safe childbirth are offered at the shrine front<ref name="ic2">伊勢文化舎（2008）：116ページ</ref><ref name="gk"/>. These torii are sold by Tawaradaya shop in Ujiurata 1-chōme<ref name="ic2-119">伊勢文化舎（2008）：119ページ</ref>.
The honden (main hall) is enclosed by a tamagaki fence. Unlike most sessha, massha, and subordinate shrines of Ise Jingu, which typically do not have offertory boxes<ref>伊勢文化舎（2008）：26ページ</ref>, Koyasu Shrine provides a saisen-bako at its front. A flower-shaped chozubachi (water basin) stands before the shrine building<ref name="ic"/>. Directly in front is Koyasu Shrine; behind it is Ōyamazumi Shrine<ref name="ic"/>.
The enshrined deity is Konohanasakuya-hime no Kami (木花開耶姫神)<ref name="ic2"/>. Originally believed to be the ujigami (tutelary deity) of Ujikan-chō<ref name="ic2"/>, she is the daughter of Ōyamazumi-no-Kami (大山祇神) enshrined at Ōyamazumi Shrine<ref name="ic"/>. She is also the wife of Ninigi-no-Mikoto (瓊瓊杵尊), said to have given birth to three children in the midst of a blazing fire<ref name="ic2"/><ref name="gk"/>. From this myth, people pray here for safe childbirth, child conception, match-making, and warding off misfortune<ref name="ic2"/><ref name="gk"/>. Other Ise Jingu shrines known for such worldly faith include {{ill|Yuta Shrine|de|Yuta-Schrein|ja|湯田神社|zh|湯田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|de|Sonai-Schrein|ja|園相神社|zh|园相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|de|Katada-Schrein|ja|堅田神社|zh|堅田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|de|Akasaki-Schrein|ja|赤崎神社|zh|赤崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="st">櫻井（1991）：294ページ</ref>.
==History== The date of foundation is unknown. It is not listed in the Engishiki nor the Enryaku gishikichō, suggesting it did not exist in ancient times<ref name="st-226-272">櫻井（1991）：266, 272ページ</ref>.
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Divinities removed from Ise Jingu’s jurisdiction all shrines not recorded in the two ancient texts, causing Ōyamazumi Shrine—sharing grounds with Koyasu Shrine—to lose its status<ref name="st"/>. The shrine administration of Ise Jingu later negotiated with the Shrine Bureau, and in 1900 (Meiji 33) the parishioners of Ōyamazumi Shrine petitioned for reinstatement. Their request was accepted, and Ōyamazumi Shrine along with its subordinate Koyasu Shrine and the combined grounds of approximately 174 tsubo (≈575 m²) were reabsorbed into the Inner Shrine in 1900<ref>櫻井（1991）：274ページ</ref>.
In March 1924 (Taishō 11), the shrine was rebuilt<ref name="uy"/>.
==Access== * By bus: From the south exit of {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|qid=Q6079748|lt=Ise Station|1=Iseshi Station}} (JR Sangu Line) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|en|Ujiyamada Station|qid=Q7878210|lt=Ujiyamada Station|1=Ujiyamada Station}} (Kintetsu Yamada/Toba Line), take the Mie Kotsu bus to Naikū-mae (Inner Shrine entrance), approx. 15 minutes<ref name="mkr">三重県観光連盟 “[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html 伊勢神宮（内宮）の観光施設・周辺情報 - 観光三重]” (accessed September 12 2013)</ref>. Buses also run from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|en|Isuzugawa Station|qid=Q6089533|lt=Isuzugawa Station|1=Isuzugawa Station}} on the Toba Line. * By car: From {{ill|Ise-Nishi IC|de|Ise-West Autobahnanschlussstelle|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|zh|伊势西交流道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10884865}} on the Ise Expressway, take Route 32 south about 5 minutes, or from {{ill|Ise IC|de|Ise-Autobahnanschlussstelle|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|zh|伊势交流道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10884857}} via National Route 23 south for about 5 minutes. Parking is available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
